response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web software developer with strengths in TypeScript, Node.js, and D, but the role requires deep hardware expertise: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. Aside from brief C/C++ work at National Instruments and some low-latency DLang services, there is no evidence of silicon architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, or performance modeling for hardware. The profile does not match the principal-level hardware focus of this position.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services in DLang)
Broad engineering background spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded work
Solid software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions)
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/application development, not principal-level hardware performance engineering

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No AI accelerator or deep-learning workload expertise
